channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: You can now watch a full Metallica concert on Apple Vision Pro
item.description: You don’t need tickets to see Metallica in concert anymore—just a $3,499 Apple Vision Pro headset. Starting today, Apple Vision Pro users can experience Metallica’s 2024 Mexico City concert as an immersive, ultrahigh resolution experience complete with 180-degree video and Spatial Audio.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: SpaceX launches a new crew to the space station to replace NASA's stuck astronauts
item.description: The replacements for NASA's two stuck astronauts are on their way to the International Space Station.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Vance hopeful 'high-level' TikTok deal will be completed by early April
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said Friday that he was hopeful a deal to keep TikTok operating in the U.S. will be wrapped up by the early April deadline.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: UN report reveals alarming extent of Iran’s increased surveillance of women
item.description: Iran increasingly relies on electronic surveillance and the public to inform on women refusing to wear the country’s mandatory headscarf in public, even as hard-liners push for harsher penalties for those protesting the law, a United Nations report released Friday found.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Refocusing CISA under Trump: A strategic approach to critical infrastructure defense
item.description: Refocusing CISA's efforts promises to defend against current threats while anticipating and mitigating future challenges, securing America's cyber future. The post Refocusing CISA under Trump: A strategic approach to critical infrastructure defense first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: You can now use an IPv4 address as business collateral - and it could be worth millions
item.description: You can now secure a loan using your IPv4 address as collateral – you’ll even still be able to access and use it.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Still can't get a Fujifilm X100VI? This premium Leica compact costs less, and it's in stock
item.description: The Leica D-Lux 8 is premium compact camera with plenty of appeal, and unlike Fujifilm and Canon rivals, it's currently in stock.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: IDC: 80% of companies plan to buy AI PCs this year
item.description: AI PCs could solve key issues organizations face when using cloud and data center AI instances, including cost, security, and privacy concerns, according to a new study by IDC Research. Nearly all organizations are already using or planning to use cloud-based AI platforms.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Volt Typhoon threat group had access to American utility networks for the best part of a year
item.description: 2023 saw the Chinese threat group Volt Typhoon get access to public power and water utilities in Massachusetts for 10 months.
item.dc:creator: luke.hughes@futurenet.com (Luke Hughes), TechRadar

item.title: I’ve tried the new Mercedes-Benz Superscreen – and its Google Gemini-powered smarts push EV infotainment to the next level
item.description: With an advanced AI assistant, Google Gemini onboard and a vast array of screens, the upcoming CLA is "the cleverest" Mercedes to date.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Social media addicts: Here are tips to stop wasting time on apps
item.description: If you’ve got a smartphone, you probably spend too much time on it — checking Instagram, watching silly TikTok videos, messaging on WhatsApp or doomscrolling on X. It can be hard to curb excessive use of smartphones and social media, which are addictive by design.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: The future of AI isn’t the model—it’s the system
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Google Pushes Back Against Age Verification Bill in Utah
item.description: Google argues that app stores and developers should share responsibility for verifying users' ages, rather than solely relying on app stores to do so.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Meta to start testing crowd-sourced fact-checking, based on X example, next week
item.description: acebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms Inc. said Thursday it will begin testing its crowd-sourced fact-checking program, Community Notes, on March 18.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Greece says ambitious Mediterranean power cable project is on track despite setbacks
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — The future of an ambitious electricity cable linking the power grids of Greece, Cyprus and Israel by the end of the decade is on track despite a series of setbacks, Greece’s Foreign Minister Giorgos Gerapetritis said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Meta to start testing crowd-sourced fact-checking, based on X example, next week
item.description: Facebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms Inc. said Thursday it will begin testing its crowd-sourced fact-checking program, Community Notes, on March 18. It will initially based on a ratings system used by Elon Musk's X. Meta ended its fact-checking program in January.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Apple faces UK antitrust threat as regulator targets browsers
item.description: Apple faces a renewed threat in the UK, where a new report from the top antitrust regulator says both it and Google are holding back innovation in the mobile browser market. By far, most of the criticism is being leveled at Apple.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: French publishers and authors sue Meta over copyright works used in AI training
item.description: French publishers and authors said Wednesday they're taking Meta to court, accusing the social media company of using their works without permission to train its artificial intelligence model.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Designing a dynamic web application with Astro
item.description: Astro.js is among the most popular meta-frameworks for JavaScript developers, and for good reason. Astro makes it relatively easy to structure web applications for maximum effect with minimal effort.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: White House nominates top leaders for CISA, other DHS components
item.description: Sean Plankey has been tapped to lead CISA after serving in key cyber roles during Trump's first term. His nomination comes as CISA grapples with recent cuts. The post White House nominates top leaders for CISA, other DHS components first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works. Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How genomics and AI could help treat cancer more effectively
item.description: “These discoveries highlight how genetic profiling can play a crucial role in personalizing cancer care,” Ruishan Liu said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation
